page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, utilizing x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, delivering high-speed local data access. It is network and EBS optimized, providing a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The combination of high-speed local storage and substantial network throughput supports data-intensive applications.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 123839.46 and a PassMark CPU score of 86426.81, placing it in the top tier. Memory performance is also strong, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 331.59 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 135.26 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a Redis SET performance of 108.32 million operations per second and strong PassMark database operation scores.

Given its massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, the r6idn.32xlarge is designed for memory-intensive and data-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, real-time analytics, and data warehousing. The high resource density of memory, compute, and local storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads that require high throughput and low latency without the need for external storage overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights its strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 86426.81 and a Redis SET throughput of 108.32 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 331.59 GB/sec for large block reads. This hardware profile makes the r6idn.32xlarge exceptionally suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-performance data processing applications requiring significant memory capacity and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-performance, memory-optimized platform featuring massive DDR4 capacity, fast local NVMe storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024 GB of DDR4 memory with 8.0 GB per core allocation
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and data warehousing
